commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=90fdf32e5887adeba5b91dfd3f369df6abc91716)
commands.review_summary:
The PR guards Windows SIGUSR1 in gateway process signaling and changes unmanaged Windows gateway restart to send SIGTERM instead, with focused unit tests.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main sends SIGUSR1 to a verified unmanaged gateway PID on Windows, and Node documents Windows signal limitations for SIGUSR1/SIGTERM. I did not run a live Windows repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor revision and real Windows proof are needed; this is not a ClawSweeper repair candidate while the external PR still needs contributor-provided real environment proof.

target.title: fix: handle SIGUSR1 restart on Windows where the signal is unsupported
target.body:
## Summary

On Windows, `process.kill(pid, "SIGUSR1")` throws `TypeError [ERR_UNKNOWN_SIGNAL]: Unknown signal: SIGUSR1` because SIGUSR1 is a Unix-only signal. This broke `openclaw gateway restart` for unmanaged gateway processes on Windows.

## Changes

### `src/infra/gateway-processes.ts`
- `signalVerifiedGatewayPidSync` now throws a clear, actionable error when SIGUSR1 is used on win32 instead of crashing with an obscure Node.js `ERR_UNKNOWN_SIGNAL` error.

### `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts`
- `restartGatewayWithoutServiceManager` now sends `SIGTERM` on Windows instead of `SIGUSR1`. The gateway SIGTERM handler already reads the restart intent file (written before signaling) and triggers a draining restart when the intent is present. This is simpler than the schtasks approach and works without a Windows scheduled task installed.

## Test plan
- [x] Added unit test: `gateway-processes.test.ts` — rejects SIGUSR1 on Windows
- [x] Added unit test: `lifecycle.test.ts` — uses SIGTERM for unmanaged gateway restart on Windows
- [x] Existing test pinned to linux platform for deterministic behavior

## Real behavior proof

### Before (broken)
```
openclaw gateway restart
Gateway restart failed: TypeError [ERR_UNKNOWN_SIGNAL]: Unknown signal: SIGUSR1
```

### After (fixed — patched dist for verification)
The patched code sends `SIGTERM` on Windows. The gateway SIGTERM handler reads the restart intent file and routes to `request("restart", "SIGTERM", ...)` when the intent is present. SIGTERM + restart intent = restart (not stop).
